title: AFL Fuzzing Series
summary: A chain of 5 papers advancing coverage-based greybox fuzzing. AFL establishes the core coverage-guided fuzzing algorithm. AFLFast models fuzzing as a Markov chain and introduces power schedules to bias exploration towards low-frequency paths. AFLGo adds directed fuzzing via distance computation and simulated annealing. FairFuzz targets rare branches through mutation masking. AFL++ combines these advances into a modular extensible framework with custom mutator API and MOpt mutation scheduling.
instructionPreview: Implement a series of 5 coverage-guided greybox fuzzing algorithms as a Python simulation framework. Working directory: /workspace. The base `fuzzsim/` package provides program model, coverage bitmap, mutation operators, and simulated target programs. Implement: (1) AFL core fuzzer with full deterministic stages (bitflip/arith/interest/dict), havoc, splice, queue management with calibration and trimming, corpus culling via greedy set cover, crash deduplication, fork server simulation, and constant power schedule; (2) AFLFast Markov chain power schedules (FAST, COE, EXPLORE, EXPLOIT, LINEAR, QUAD) with path frequency tracking, state transition modeling, and seed prioritization strategies; (3) AFLGo directed fuzzing with call-graph and CFG distance computation (harmonic mean), distance normalization, simulated annealing with multiple cooling schedules (exponential/linear/logarithmic/adaptive), and distance-aware seed prioritization; (4) FairFuzz rare branch targeting with dynamic threshold computation, probe-based influence detection, mutation mask generation with caching, and targeted havoc preserving influential bytes; (5) AFL++ custom mutator API (init/fuzz/havoc_mutation/ post_process/trim/queue_new_entry), MutatorRegistry with dynamic weights, GrammarMutator, CmpLog/RedQueen mutator, MOpt PSO-based mutation scheduling (pilot/core phases with velocity/position updates), and PowerScheduleEnsemble rotation. Each module's run.sh section outputs EXPERIMENT_NAME: VALUE lines. Tests verify deterministic results with seed=42.
instruction:
Implement a series of 5 coverage-guided greybox fuzzing algorithms as a Python simulation framework.
Working directory: /workspace. The base `fuzzsim/` package provides program model, coverage bitmap,
mutation operators, and simulated target programs. Implement: (1) AFL core fuzzer with full deterministic
stages (bitflip/arith/interest/dict), havoc, splice, queue management with calibration and trimming,
corpus culling via greedy set cover, crash deduplication, fork server simulation, and constant power
schedule; (2) AFLFast Markov chain power schedules (FAST, COE, EXPLORE, EXPLOIT, LINEAR, QUAD) with
path frequency tracking, state transition modeling, and seed prioritization strategies; (3) AFLGo
directed fuzzing with call-graph and CFG distance computation (harmonic mean), distance normalization,
simulated annealing with multiple cooling schedules (exponential/linear/logarithmic/adaptive), and
distance-aware seed prioritization; (4) FairFuzz rare branch targeting with dynamic threshold
computation, probe-based influence detection, mutation mask generation with caching, and targeted
havoc preserving influential bytes; (5) AFL++ custom mutator API (init/fuzz/havoc_mutation/
post_process/trim/queue_new_entry), MutatorRegistry with dynamic weights, GrammarMutator,
CmpLog/RedQueen mutator, MOpt PSO-based mutation scheduling (pilot/core phases with velocity/position
updates), and PowerScheduleEnsemble rotation.
Each module's run.sh section outputs EXPERIMENT_NAME: VALUE lines. Tests verify deterministic results
with seed=42.

moduleDag.description: A chain of 5 papers advancing coverage-based greybox fuzzing. AFL establishes the core coverage-guided fuzzing algorithm. AFLFast models fuzzing as a Markov chain and introduces power schedules to bias exploration towards low-frequency paths. AFLGo adds directed fuzzing via distance computation and simulated annealing. FairFuzz targets rare branches through mutation masking. AFL++ combines these advances into a modular extensible framework with custom mutator API and MOpt mutation scheduling.

nodes.label: AFL / American Fuzzy Lop
nodes.description: Core coverage-based greybox fuzzer with 64KB edge coverage bitmap, hit count bucketing, fork server simulation, queue management with calibration, corpus culling via greedy set cover, full deterministic stages (bitflip 1/2/4, byte flip 8/16/32, arithmetic 8/16/32, interesting values, dictionary), havoc stage with random stacked mutations, splice, trimming, and crash deduplication via coverage hashing.

nodes.label: AFLFast: Coverage-based Greybox Fuzzing as Markov Chain
nodes.description: Markov chain model of fuzzing with path frequency tracking and state transitions. Power schedules (EXPLOIT, EXPLORE, COE, FAST, LINEAR, QUAD) that assign energy inversely proportional to path frequency f(i) and exponentially in selection count s(i). PathFrequencyTracker for Markov state identification, transition probability estimation, and rare path detection. Seed prioritization strategies and energy distribution analysis.

nodes.label: AFLGo: Directed Greybox Fuzzing
nodes.description: Directed fuzzing with two-level distance computation: function-level BFS on call graph and basic-block level via harmonic mean. CallGraph and CFGDistance classes for graph construction. Distance normalization to [0,1]. Simulated annealing with multiple cooling schedules (exponential, linear, logarithmic, adaptive). Temperature-based exploration/exploitation transition, distance-aware energy assignment, and seed skipping during exploitation.

nodes.label: FairFuzz: Targeted Mutation for Rare Branches
nodes.description: Rare branch identification via edge frequency tracking with both fixed and dynamic (percentile-based) thresholds. RareBranchTracker with incremental updates. Probe-based influence detection (differential mutation analysis) to determine which input bytes control target branches. InfluenceDetector with caching. Mutation masking that preserves influential bytes during targeted havoc mutations.

nodes.label: AFL++: Combining Incremental Steps of Fuzzing Research
nodes.description: Full custom mutator API (init/fuzz/fuzz_count/havoc_mutation/post_process/ trim/queue_new_entry/describe). MutatorRegistry with dynamic weight adjustment. GrammarMutator (template-based structured inputs). CmpLogMutator (RedQueen comparison operand replacement). MOpt PSO-based mutation scheduling with pilot/core phases, velocity/position updates, multi-swarm support, and convergence tracking. PowerScheduleEnsemble for rotating through schedule strategies.

edges.label: AFLFast replaces AFL's constant power schedule
FairFuzz adds rare branch targeted mutation
